paper_title: PhaseNAS: Language-Model Driven Architecture Search with Dynamic Phase Adaptation
problem_statement: Current LLM-based neural architecture search methods use static configurations throughout the search process, leading to resource inefficiency and poor performance in complex tasks.
key_limitations: Resource Mismatch in Search Phases: Fixed LLM configurations regardless of search phase requirements
Static Resource Allocation: Lack of adaptive mechanisms to match computational resources to search phase complexity
Ambiguous Architecture Representation: Rigid or specially-tokenized architecture descriptions causing comprehension challenges for LLMs

proposed_solution.name: PhaseNAS
proposed_solution.description: A dynamic, LLM-based neural architecture search framework with phase-aware resource allocation
proposed_solution.approach: Two-phase approach: initial broad exploration followed by refinement phase

application_domains: Complex perception tasks (object detection) in unmanned systems

key_contributions.name: Phase-Aware Dynamic Search
key_contributions.description: Introduces a phase-aware controller that adaptively alternates between broad exploration and focused refinement, guided by the distribution of top-performing architectures

key_contributions.name: Unified Architecture Template Language
key_contributions.description: Proposes a structured, parameterized template language to bridge natural language prompts with executable network components

title: PhaseNAS: Language-Model Driven Architecture Search with Dynamic Phase Adaptation
